Tests if multiplying NaN by positive infinity results in NaN using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion that tests the behavior of multiplying `Number.NaN` by `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` in JavaScript. The assertion checks that the result of this operation is `NaN` by using the `isNaN` function, and it expects the result to be `true`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles the multiplication of `NaN` with positive infinity, which is a part of the IEEE 754 floating-point arithmetic standard. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific mathematical operation and its expected outcome.
